questions.q: In which Indian state is Mysore Palace located?
questions.explanation: Mysore Palace stands in the city of Mysuru (Mysore) in the southern Indian state of Karnataka.

questions.q: Mysore Palace was the seat of which royal family?
questions.explanation: Mysore Palace served as the official residence and seat of the Wodeyar dynasty, the royal family of the Kingdom of Mysore.

questions.q: Mysore Palace is most famous for being illuminated during which festival?
questions.explanation: During the Dasara festival the palace is lit with tens of thousands of bulbs, a tradition that draws crowds from across the world.

questions.q: In which city is Mysore Palace located?
questions.explanation: The palace is located in Mysuru (Mysore), historically the cultural capital of Karnataka.

questions.q: What type of monument is Mysore Palace?
questions.explanation: Mysore Palace is a royal palace, the grand residence of the Wodeyar kings of Mysore.

questions.q: Mysore Palace is one of the most visited tourist attractions in India. What broadly draws visitors to it?
options: Its opulent architecture and Dasara illumination
questions.explanation: Visitors are drawn by the palace's opulent interiors, ornate halls, and the spectacular Dasara lighting.

questions.q: The lavish nighttime lighting of Mysore Palace is created using what?
questions.explanation: The palace facade is outlined by roughly a hundred thousand light bulbs that switch on during Dasara and on special occasions.

questions.q: Mysore Palace is also commonly known by which name?
questions.explanation: The palace is formally known as Amba Vilas Palace, after the goddess Amba (Chamundeshwari) revered by the royal family.

questions.q: Mysore Palace overlooks which sacred hill associated with the city?
questions.explanation: The palace faces the Chamundi Hill, home to the Chamundeshwari Temple, the guardian deity of the Wodeyars.

questions.q: Mysore Palace is best described as an example of which broad category of Indian heritage?
questions.explanation: The present palace is an early 20th-century royal building, a prime example of grand princely-era palace architecture.

questions.q: The present Mysore Palace is built in which predominant architectural style?
questions.explanation: The palace is a celebrated example of the Indo-Saracenic style, blending Hindu, Islamic, Rajput, and Gothic elements.

questions.q: Which British architect designed the present Mysore Palace?
questions.explanation: The current palace was designed by the British architect Henry Irwin and completed in the early 20th century.

questions.q: Why was the present Mysore Palace built in the early 1900s?
options: It replaced an earlier palace destroyed by fire
It was built for a Mughal emperor
It was a British military barracks
It was originally a temple complex
questions.explanation: The earlier wooden palace was largely destroyed by fire in 1897, and the present stone palace was built to replace it.

questions.q: Construction of the present Mysore Palace was completed in which year?
questions.explanation: The reconstruction of the palace was completed in 1912 under the rule of Maharaja Krishnaraja Wodeyar IV.

questions.q: Under which Maharaja was the present Mysore Palace constructed?
questions.explanation: The palace was commissioned during the reign of Maharaja Krishnaraja Wodeyar IV, whose mother oversaw much of the rebuilding.

questions.q: The grand Durbar Hall and Kalyana Mantapa are notable interior spaces of which kind in the palace?
questions.explanation: The Durbar Hall (royal audience hall) and the Kalyana Mantapa (marriage pavilion) are among the palace's grandest ceremonial spaces.

questions.q: The Kalyana Mantapa of Mysore Palace is especially famous for which feature?
options: A stained-glass domed ceiling with peacock motifs
questions.explanation: The octagonal Kalyana Mantapa is crowned by a stunning stained-glass dome decorated with peacock motifs.

questions.q: The famous golden throne kept at Mysore Palace is displayed publicly mainly during which occasion?
questions.explanation: The royal golden throne (the Chinnada Simhasana) is assembled and displayed during the Dasara celebrations.

questions.q: What event during Mysore Dasara features a richly decorated elephant procession?
questions.explanation: The Jamboo Savari, in which a caparisoned elephant carries the idol of Chamundeshwari, is the highlight of Mysore Dasara.

questions.q: Mysore Palace stands within a fortified enclosure that also contains several temples. What does this reflect?
options: Its origin as a Portuguese fort
Its use as a colonial warehouse
The Wodeyars' devotion and the palace's role as a royal-religious complex
Its design as a purely military citadel
questions.explanation: The palace complex is enclosed by a fort and includes several temples, reflecting the Wodeyars' religious devotion and the site's royal-ceremonial role.

questions.q: The materials used in the present Mysore Palace prominently include which of the following?
options: Fine grey granite with domes and marble
questions.explanation: The palace was built largely of fine grey granite with deep pink marble domes, giving it a durable and ornate finish.

questions.q: Mysore Palace is today maintained and managed by which kind of body?
options: A government-administered board (the Mysore Palace Board)
questions.explanation: The palace is administered by the Mysore Palace Board under the Karnataka government, with parts open as a museum to the public.

questions.q: The original Mysore fort and earlier palace site is historically linked to the founding rulers of the Wodeyar dynasty in which century?
questions.explanation: The Wodeyar dynasty rose to prominence around the 14th century, and a palace has stood on the site through successive rebuildings since then.

questions.q: During the late 18th century, control of Mysore passed for a time to rulers other than the Wodeyars. Who were they?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali and his son Tipu Sultan ruled Mysore in the late 18th century before the Wodeyars were restored by the British in 1799.

questions.q: The princely regent who oversaw much of the early reconstruction of the present palace was which royal figure?
questions.explanation: Maharani Vani Vilas Sannidhana (Kempananjammanni), regent during the minority of Krishnaraja Wodeyar IV, played a key role in commissioning the rebuilding.

questions.q: The Indo-Saracenic style of Mysore Palace is characterized by what combination of features?
options: Flying buttresses and pointed Gothic spires only
Domes, arches, and turrets blended with Indian temple motifs
questions.explanation: Indo-Saracenic architecture combines Islamic-style domes, arches, and turrets with Hindu and Rajput decorative elements, all visible at Mysore Palace.

questions.q: The tall central tower of Mysore Palace is crowned by a gilded dome of roughly what height above the ground?
questions.explanation: The palace's five-storey central tower rises to about 145 feet, topped by a gilded dome that dominates the skyline.

questions.q: Which deity, worshipped by the Wodeyars, gives the palace its formal name Amba Vilas?
questions.explanation: The palace is named Amba Vilas after Amba, a form of the goddess Chamundeshwari, the tutelary deity of the Wodeyar kings.

questions.q: A celebrated artistic feature inside Mysore Palace is a large collection of paintings depicting which subject?
options: The Mysore Dasara procession and royal life
questions.explanation: The palace houses a famous series of paintings, including detailed depictions of the Mysore Dasara procession and royal ceremonies.

questions.q: After India's independence, what became of Mysore Palace and the Wodeyar royal status?
options: It was demolished for a railway station
The palace became a state-managed heritage site while the family retained part as residence
It was sold to a foreign government
It was converted entirely into a military fort
questions.explanation: Following the integration of princely states, much of the palace became a government-administered heritage and museum site, while the royal family retained a portion as residence.
